top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 81, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Granfondo do Porto's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 81 miles.
64ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo do Porto's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
At Granfondo do Porto, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo do Porto. Train it until it feels boring.
5,200ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 81 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Granfondo do Porto, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
5,200ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 81 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.heat: April in Porto, Portugal can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Granfondo do Porto. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Granfondo do Porto’s 81 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Granfondo do Porto. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Granfondo do Porto requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 81 miles of mixed terrain in Porto, Portugal.
